summary: Make `--full` and `--latest-only` restart hierarchical resources from the head. `syncResource` now ignores the stored resume cursor whenever full or latestOnly is set, instead of relying on caller-side clearing loops that only zeroed top-level sync_state keys.
reason: Greptile findings on PR #433. The hierarchical-sync-traversal patch keys resume cursors per (resource, parentID) as `resource:parentID`. Both the `--full` clearing loop and the `--latest-only` clearing loop in `newSyncCmd` only called SaveSyncState/GetSyncState on the bare `resource` key, never the per-parent keys. If a prior hierarchical sync was interrupted mid-resource, `--full` and `--latest-only` left those `resource:parentID` cursors intact and `syncResource` resumed affected lists from page N instead of page 0 — contradicting `--full`'s documented 'ignore previous checkpoint' and `--latest-only`'s 'refresh the head'. Fixed at the point of use: `syncResource` takes a `latestOnly` parameter and zeroes the cursor whenever `full || latestOnly`, covering every target — global and per-parent alike. The redundant and incomplete caller-side clearing loops are removed; `newWorkflowArchiveCmd` passes latestOnly=false since `workflow archive` has no such flag.
